An eruption; the sudden appearance of a rash, disease, etc.

examples.english: The next four to six weeks are crucial in controlling the massive Ebola outbreak.

current_entries.en_definition: A sudden increase.
(figurative) An outburst or sudden eruption, especially of violence and mischief.
Occurring between dusk and dawn.

examples.english: English cannot be mastered overnight.

current_entries.en_definition: Complete before the next morning.
For which participants stay overnight.
(figurative) In a very short (but unspecified) amount of time.
(botany) The structure in a plant that develops into a seed after fertilization; the megasporangium of a seed plant with its enclosing integuments.
(zoology) An immature ovum in mammals.
The combination of a substance with oxygen.

examples.english: Full oxidation gives black tea its deep, robust flavor and dark amber color.

current_entries.en_definition: (chemistry) A reaction in which the atoms of an element lose electrons and the oxidation state of the element increases.
(inorganic chemistry) An allotrope of oxygen (symbol O‚āÉ) having three atoms in the molecule instead of the usual two; it is a toxic gas, generated from oxygen by electrical discharge.
(Britain, informal) Fresh air, especially that breathed at the seaside and smelling of seaweed.
(phonology, uncountable) The state or quality of being palatalized, of pronouncing a sound with the tongue against the palate of the mouth that normally is not.
(phonology, countable) An instance of pronunciation in which a sound is palatalized.
The study of old or ancient forms of writing.
Ancient scripts or forms of writing themselves, as uncial, scriptio continua, or methods of using papyrus scrolls.
(sometimes, narrowly) The West Bank and Gaza Strip.

(historical, technical, rare) The British League of Nations Mandate for Palestine, of which this region was a part (the remainder being Transjordan, which covered approximately the lands of the modern Kingdom (originally Emirate) of Jordan).
A remedy believed to cure all disease and prolong life that was originally sought by alchemists; a cure-all.
A solution to all problems.

(Greek mythology) The first woman on earth, who had been given a box by the gods and instructed not to open it, but who disobeyed the instructions out of curiosity, releasing all manner of evils into the world.
(astronomy) 55 Pandora, a main belt asteroid.
A picture or series of pictures representing a continuous scene.
(figuratively) A comprehensive survey.
A mechanical linkage based on parallelograms causing two objects to move in parallel; notably as a drawing aid.
(rail transport) A similarly-formed conductive device, now usually Z-shaped, that collects electric current from overhead lines for trains and trams.
A small, decorative, somewhat weighty object placed on one or more pieces of paper to keep them from fluttering away.
Any object used for this purpose.
(usually, uncountable) A plant (Cyperus papyrus) in the sedge family, native to the Nile river valley, paper reed.
(usually, uncountable) A material similar to paper made from the papyrus plant.
A scroll or document written on papyrus.
A pattern, a way of doing something; especially a pattern of thought, a system of beliefs, a conceptual framework.
(linguistics) A set of all forms which contain a common element, especially the set of all inflectional forms of a word or a particular grammatical category.
An example serving as the model for such a pattern; an exceptionally good or prototypical example of a pattern or group.
A value kept constant during an experiment, equation, calculation, or similar, but varied over other versions of the experiment, equation, calculation, etc.
(programming) An input variable of a function definition, that gets an actual value (argument) at execution time.

The obsolete name for a psychotic disorder, now called delusional disorder, often (in one of six subtypes) characterized by delusions of persecution and a perceived threat against the individual affected with the disorder, and often associated with false accusations and a general mistrust of others
Extreme, irrational distrust of others.

(biology) An organism that lives on or in another organism of a different species, deriving benefit from living on or in that other organism, while not contributing towards that other organism sufficiently to cover the cost to that other organism.

examples.english: Tom is a parasite.

current_entries.en_definition: (pejorative) A person who lives on other people's efforts or expense and gives little or nothing back. [from 16th c.]
(transitive) To dry to extremity; to shrivel with heat.
(transitive, colloquial) To make very thirsty.
A document made on such material.
Stiff paper imitating that material.
A clause, phrase or word which is inserted (usually for explanation or amplification) into a passage which is already grammatically complete, and usually marked off with brackets, commas or dashes.
Either of a pair of brackets, especially (mainly US) round brackets, ( and ) (used to enclose parenthetical material in a text).
(rhetoric) A digression; the use of such digressions.
The capital and largest city of France.

examples.english: Paris is the most beautiful city in the world.

current_entries.en_definition: A department of √éle-de-France, France.
A city, the county seat of Monroe County, Missouri; named for the city in Kentucky.
(Greek mythology) A Trojan prince who eloped with Helen.
(Roman Catholicism, Anglican, Lutheranism, Eastern Orthodox) An administrative part of a diocese that has its own church.
The community attending that church; the members of the parish.

(US) An ecclesiastical society, usually not bounded by territorial limits, but composed of those persons who choose to unite under the charge of a particular priest, clergyman, or minister; also, loosely, the territory in which the members of a congregation live.
A civil subdivision of a British county, often corresponding to an earlier ecclesiastical parish.
A space in which a car or other vehicle can be parked. (Sometimes demarcated with lines painted on the ground.)
(uncountable, uncommon) Space in which a car or other vehicle can be parked.

In many countries, the legislative branch of government, a deliberative assembly or set of assemblies whose elected or appointed members meet to debate the major political issues of the day, make, amend, and repeal laws, authorize the executive branch of government to collect and spend money, and in some cases exercise judicial powers; a legislature. [from 14th c.]

A particular assembly of the members of such a legislature, as convened for a specific purpose or period of time (commonly designated with an ordinal number ‚Äď for example, first parliament or 12th parliament ‚Äď or a descriptive adjective ‚Äď for example, Long Parliament, Short Parliament and Rump Parliament). [from 14th c.]
(dated, except in, Mid-Atlantic US) The living room of a house, or a room for entertaining guests; a room for talking; a sitting-room or drawing room.
A shop or other business selling goods or services specified by context.
A province in Emilia-Romagna, Italy.

examples.english: He is a student, he studies Chinese at the Universit√† degli Studi di Parma.

current_entries.en_definition: A city in Canyon County, Idaho.
A city in Cuyahoga County, Ohio.
A biennial plant, Pastinaca sativa, related to the carrot.
The root of the parsnip, when used as a vegetable.
Relating to Parthia or Parthians.
A native or inhabitant of Parthia.
An extinct Western Iranian language that was spoken in Parthia.
A very small piece of matter, a fragment; especially, the smallest possible part of something. [from 14th c.]
(physics) Any of various physical objects making up the constituent parts of an atom; an elementary particle or subatomic particle. [from 19th c.]

(grammar) A part of speech that has no inherent lexical definition but must be associated with another word to impart meaning, often a grammatical category: for example, the English word to in a full infinitive phrase (to eat) or O in a vocative phrase (O Canada), or as a discourse marker (mmm).
A covered walkway, between rooms or buildings.
Any way for passing in, out or through something.
Any great, strong, powerful emotion, especially romantic love or extreme hate.

examples.english: To share one's passion is to live it fully.

current_entries.en_definition: An object of passionate or romantic love or strong romantic interest.
(military, security) A word relayed to a person to gain admittance to a place or to gain access to information.

examples.english: The password is "Muiriel".

current_entries.en_definition: (computing, cryptography) A string of characters used to log in to a computer or network, to access a level in a video game, and so on; archetypally a word#Noun but nowadays often an alphanumeric#Adjective string or a phrase#Noun.
An often round and somewhat flat flavoured candy or sweet.
Any small, usually round and somewhat flat, granular piece of material; a tablet.
(historical) A medicinal pill, originally made of compressed herbs.
Any of several East Indian plants in the genus Pogostemon, especially Pogostemon cablin, which yield a highly fragrant oil.
The oil or perfume made from these plants.
A sister of someone‚Äôs father.
The wife of a sibling of someone‚Äôs father.
(academic, _, medicine) The academic biomedical specialty that advances the aspects of the biomedical sciences that allow for those clinical applications and their advancements over time.
Pathosis: any deviation from a healthy or normal structure or function; abnormality; illness or malformation.

(Christianity) The highest form of bishop, in the ancient world having authority over other bishops in the province but now generally as an honorary title; in Roman Catholicism, considered a bishop second only to the Pope in rank. [from 9th c.]
The male head of a household or nuclear family.
The name of someone's father.
A patronymic surname.
Derived from one's father.
(by extension) Derived from one's ancestors.

(onomastics, by extension) A name acquired from the first name of one's father, grandfather or earlier (male) ancestor. Some cultures use a patronymic where other cultures use a surname or family name; other cultures (like Russia) use both a patronymic and a surname.
A province of Lombardy, Italy.
The capital city of the province of Pavia, Lombardy, Italy.
A municipality in Iloilo province, Philippines.
An ornate tent.
A light roofed structure used as a shelter in a public place.
A structure, sometimes temporary, erected to house exhibits at a fair, etc.
A detached or semi-detached building at a hospital or other building complex.
The act of paying.

examples.english: We will make the payment by bank transfer.

current_entries.en_definition: An instance of that act; a sum of money paid in exchange#Noun for goods or services, or the transaction#Noun that conveys it.
(especially, when without a qualifier) A plant, Pisum sativum, member of the legume (Fabaceae) family.

examples.english: There's a pea in my right nostril.

current_entries.en_definition: Any of certain plants of the family Fabaceae: Pisum sativum and others.
(culinary) The edible seed of Pisum sativum.
(culinary) The edible seed of various other pea plants.
A state of tranquility, quiet, and harmony. For instance, a state free from civil disturbance.

examples.english: We are longing for world peace.

current_entries.en_definition: Harmony in personal relations.
A state free of war, in particular war between different countries.
A peafowl (of the genus Pavo or Afropavo), either male or female.
(entomology) Any of various Asian species of papilionid butterflies of the genus Papilio.

A member of the lowly social class that toils on the land, constituted by small farmers and tenants, sharecroppers, farmhands and other laborers on the land where they form the main labor force in agriculture and horticulture.
A country person.
(pejorative) An uncouth, crude or ill-bred person.
(strategy games) A worker unit.
A person who makes an excessive or tedious show of their knowledge, especially regarding rules of vocabulary and grammar.
A person who is overly concerned with formal rules and trivial points of learning.
Being overly concerned with formal rules and trivial points of learning, like a pedant.
Tending to show off one‚Äôs knowledge, often in a tiresome manner.
Erotic love, sexually expressed or chaste, between a man and an adolescent boy.
(archaic) Anal intercourse in general, usually between a man and an adolescent boy.
(psychiatry, sexology) An adult or older adolescent who is sexually attracted to prepubescent children. [from 20th c.]
(pejorative, by extension, common use) An adult who is sexually attracted to or engages in sexual acts with a child under the age of consent.
Sexual attraction by adults and adolescents to children, specifically prepubescent children.
(by extension, proscribed) Sexual activity between adults and children; the act of child molestation.

(Greek mythology) A winged horse fabled to have sprung from the blood of Medusa when she was slain. He is noted for causing, with a blow of his hoof, Hippocrene, the inspiring fountain of the Muses, to spring from Mount Helicon. Bellerophon tamed and rode upon Pegasus when he defeated the Chimaera.

(constellation) A constellation of the northern sky, near the vernal equinoctial point, representing the winged horse. Its three brightest stars, with the brightest star of Andromeda, form the asterism of the Great Square of Pegasus.
A partially shaded area around the edges of a shadow, especially an eclipse.
(astronomy) A region around the edge of a sunspot, darker than the sun's surface but lighter than the middle of the sunspot.

A hybrid herb of the mint family (Mentha √ó piperita), formed by crossing watermint and spearmint, which has a high menthol content and a sharp flavor and is used in cooking, especially in herb teas and in confections. [from 17th c.]
Any of various sweets containing extract of the peppermint herb; peppermint confectionery. [from 19th c.]
The organisation, identification and interpretation of sensory information.
Conscious understanding of something.
Vision (ability)
Eternal damnation.
Hell.
(intransitive) To exhibit an expected pattern of behavior; to function; to work.

examples.english: Doctors refused to perform a second operation.

current_entries.en_definition: (ambitransitive) To do (something) in front of an audience, such as acting or music, often in order to entertain.
Possibly.

examples.english: Perhaps you are right, I have been selfish.

current_entries.en_definition: (dated) By chance.
(mathematics) A one-to-one mapping from a finite set to itself.
(mathematics, combinatorics) An ordering of a finite set of distinct elements.
(music) A transformation of a set's prime form, by applying one or more of certain operations, specifically, transposition, inversion, and retrograde.

(Greek mythology) The mythological Greek warrior who slew the Gorgon Medusa by decapitating her. He married Andromeda after rescuing her from Ceto and founded Mycenae. He was the son of Zeus and Danae.
(constellation) An autumn constellation of the northern sky, said to resemble the mythical hero. It includes the stars Algenib, Algol, and Mirfak.
Collection of people in an organization, such as employees and office staff, members of the military, etc.
(non-standard, countable, plural personnel) A member of an organization such as an army.
A view, vista or outlook.
The appearance of depth in objects, especially as perceived using binocular vision.
The technique of representing three-dimensional objects on a two-dimensional surface.
(figuratively) The choice of a single angle or point of view from which to sense, categorize, measure or codify experience.
A province of Abruzzo, Italy.
The capital city of the province of Pescara, Abruzzo, Italy.
A general belief that bad things will happen.
The doctrine that this world is the worst of all possible worlds.
The corresponding part of the current-day city of Budapest, on the eastern side of the Danube.
(now, _, rare) A pestilence, i.e. a deadly epidemic, a deadly plague.
An annoying person, a nuisance.
A en given name.

examples.english: Peter loves Jane.

current_entries.en_definition: The leading Apostle in the New Testament: Saint Peter.
(biblical) The epistles of Peter in the New Testament of the Bible, 1 Peter and 2 Peter attributed to St. Peter.
(slang) The penis.
The science of medicinal substances, inclusive of pharmaceutics, pharmaceutical chemistry, pharmacology, phytochemistry, and forensics.
The role or occupation of a pharmacist.
A locale in the United States; named for the city in Pennsylvania.
A village in Brandenburg, Germany; named for the city in Pennsylvania.
One of the twelve disciples of Jesus in the Bible; one of the seven deacons.
(countable, rare, _, compared to given name) A surname originating as a patronymic.
(archaic) A person who applies the principles of philosophy to the conduct of their life, as by acting calmly and rationally in the face of inevitable change.
(archaic) A student, scholar, or expert in any branch of knowledge, especially those branches studied prior to being considered part of pure science.
An academic discipline that seeks truth through reasoning rather than empiricism, often attempting to provide explanations relating to general concepts such as existence and rationality.

examples.english: The first subject that attracted my attention was philosophy.

current_entries.en_definition: A view or outlook regarding fundamental principles underlying some domain.
A general principle (usually moral).
A comprehensive system of belief.
Relating to the sounds of spoken language.
(linguistics) Relating to phones (as opposed to phonemes).

The study of the way sounds function in languages, including accent, intonation, phonemes, stress, and syllable structure, and which sounds are distinctive units within a language; (countable) the way sounds function within a given language; a phonological system. [from late 18th c.]

(by extension, uncountable) The study of the way components of signs function in a sign language, and which components are distinctive units within the language; (countable) the way components of signs function within a given sign language.
The art and technology of producing images on photosensitive surfaces, and its digital counterpart.

examples.english: I have an interest in photography.

current_entries.en_definition: The occupation of taking (and often printing) photographs.
A short written or spoken expression.

examples.english: What is the meaning of this phrase?

current_entries.en_definition: (grammar) A word or, more commonly, a group of words that functions as a single unit in the syntax of a sentence, always containing an expressed or implied head (the principal word or subgroup, with core importance) and often consisting of a head plus some other elaborating words.
(music) A small section of music in a larger piece.
(transitive) To express (an action, thought or idea) by means of particular words.
The 16th letter of the Classical and Modern Greek alphabets and the seventeenth in Old Greek.

(mathematics) An irrational and transcendental constant representing the ratio of the circumference of a Euclidean circle to its diameter; approximately 3.14159265358979323846264338327950; usually written ŌÄ.
The flesh of a pineapple fruit used as a food item.

examples.english: You can decorate the cocktail with a cherry or a pineapple.

current_entries.en_definition: (slang) An Australian fifty dollar note.
The fictional boy made from wood whose nose gets longer with each lie he tells, protagonist of the story The Adventures of Pinocchio (1881‚Äď1883) by Carlo Collodi.
The popular fairy tale about Pinocchio.
(crime, nautical) Robbery at sea, a violation of international law; taking a ship away from the control of those who are legally entitled to it.
A similar violation of international law, such as hijacking of an aircraft.
The unauthorized duplication of goods protected by intellectual property law.
A city and comune, the capital of the province of Pistoia, Tuscany, Italy.
A province of Tuscany, Italy.
A baked Italian dish of a thinly rolled bread crust typically topped before baking with tomato sauce, cheese, and other ingredients such as meat or vegetables.

examples.english: I like pizza very much.

current_entries.en_definition: A single instance of this dish.
(now, historical, or, astrology) Each of the seven major bodies which move relative to the fixed stars in the night sky‚ÄĒthe Moon, Mercury, Venus, the Sun, Mars, Jupiter and Saturn. [from 14thc.]

examples.english: When I was your age, Pluto was a planet.

current_entries.en_definition: (astronomy, historical) Any body that orbits the Sun, including the asteroids (as minor planets) and sometimes the moons of those bodies (as satellite planets)

(astronomy, current) A body which is massive enough to be in hydrostatic equilibrium (generally resulting in being an ellipsoid) but not enough to attain nuclear fusion and, in IAU usage, which directly orbits a star (or multiple star) and dominates the region of its orbit; specifically, in the case of the Solar system, the eight major bodies of Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, and Neptune. [from 2006]

Any flat, thin piece of clay, ivory, metal, etc., used for ornament, or for painting pictures upon, as a dish, plate, slab, etc., hung upon a wall; also, a smaller decoration worn by a person, such as a brooch.
A piece of flat metal with writing on it, attached to a building, monument, or other structure to remind people of a person or an event.
(uncountable, dentistry) An accumulation of biofilm, or bacteria, on teeth, which may develop into dental calculus (tartar).
(physics) A state of matter consisting of partially ionized gas and electrons.
(hematology) A clear component of blood or lymph containing fibrin.
A raised floor for any purpose, e.g. for workmen during construction, or formerly for military cannon.

examples.english: The train for Birmingham leaves from platform 3.

current_entries.en_definition: (politics, figurative) A political stance on a broad set of issues, which are called planks.
(footwear, in the plural) Ellipsis of platform shoe (‚Äúa kind of high shoe with an extra layer between the inner and outer soles‚ÄĚ).
(computing) A particular operating system or environment such as a database or other specific software; a particular type of computer or microprocessor, used for running other software.
(radio) A list of recorded songs scheduled to be played on a radio station.
(computing) A list of tracks or videos to be played in a particular sequence, as from an audio CD or a streaming service.
A state of being pleased or contented; gratification.
A person, thing or action that causes enjoyment.
One's preference.
Consisting of or containing more than one of something.

examples.english: The plural form of "louse" is "lice".

current_entries.en_definition: (grammar, uncountable) The plural number. In English, referring to more or less than one of something.
(grammar, countable) A word in the form in which it potentially refers to something other than one person or thing; and other than two things if the language has a dual form.
(Greek god, Roman god) The Greco-Roman god of the underworld.

examples.english: When I was your age, Pluto was a planet.

current_entries.en_definition: (astronomy) The largest dwarf planet and formerly the ninth planet, represented by the symbol ‚ôá or ‚Įď, both now used mostly in astrology. [from 1930]
(by extension, ambitransitive) To take anything illegally or unfairly.
(obsolete) To stab; to pierce; to spear or drive or plunge into something.
A literary piece written in verse.
A piece of writing in the tradition of poetry, an instance of poetry.
A position from which something is perceived; outlook; standpoint.
An attitude, opinion, or set of beliefs.
(literary theory) The perspective from which a narrative is related.
Anything that points or is used for pointing.
(programming) A variable that holds the address of a memory location where a value can be stored.
A needlelike component of a timepiece or measuring device that indicates the time or the current reading of the device.
(hunting) A breed of hunting dog, trained to point out game.
The administration of a poison.

examples.english: Have you ever had food poisoning?

current_entries.en_definition: The state of being poisoned.
(transitive) To give something political characteristics; to turn into a political issue.
(transitive) To make someone politically active or aware.
(transitive) To make something harmful, especially by the addition of some unwanted product.

examples.english: We ought to do our best not to pollute our environment.

current_entries.en_definition: (transitive) To make something or somewhere less suitable for some activity, especially by the introduction of some unnatural factor.
(Greek god) One of the Dioscuri, son of Zeus and Leda, brother of Castor.
(star) A star in the constellation Gemini; beta (ő≤) Geminorum.
(dance) A stately Polish dance in triple time and moderate tempo.
(music genre) Music for this dance.

(organic chemistry) A long or larger molecule consisting of a chain or network of many repeating units, formed by chemically bonding together many identical or similar small molecules called monomers. A polymer is formed by polymerization, the joining of many monomer molecules.
A material consisting of such polymer molecules.
The ability to assume different forms or shapes.
(biology) The coexistence, in the same locality, of two or more distinct forms independent of sex, not connected by intermediate gradations, but produced from common parents.
(object-oriented programming) A feature pertaining to the dynamic treatment of data elements based on their type, allowing for a method to have several definitions.

(mathematics, type theory) The property of certain typed formal systems of allowing for the use of type variables and binders/quantifiers over those type variables; likewise, the property of certain expressions (within such typed formal systems) of making use of at least one such typed variable.
(crystallography) The ability of a solid material to exist in more than one form or crystal structure; pleomorphism.
The fruit of the Punica granatum, about the size of an orange with a thick, hard, reddish skin enclosing many seeds, each with an edible pink or red pulp tasting both sweet and tart.

examples.english: My lunch was an apple and pears and pomegranate.

current_entries.en_definition: The shrub or small tree that bears the fruit.
An apparatus for gymnastic exercises with a cylindrical body covered with leather and two upright pommels, as hand grips, near the centre; held upright with adjustable legs.
An athletic event in which the pommel horse is used.
(childish) (A piece of) excrement.
(childish) Defecation.
